# Store Carousel Iguazu Events Regression

# Store Carousel Iguazu Events Regression

**Contributors:** [Frank Zhang](mailto:frank.zhang@doordash.com)[Muxi Xu](mailto:muxi.xu@doordash.com)[Michael Chen](mailto:michael.chen@doordash.com)[Daniel Fonyo](mailto:daniel.fonyo@doordash.com)

### What Happened

Store carousel Iguazu events (`CX_CROSS_VERTICAL_HOME_PAGE_FEED_ICE`) stopped being emitted for the vast majority of store carousels on the consumer homepage feed starting January 21, 2026\. Impact scaled with the [gating Andromeda DV](https://ops.doordash.team/decision-systems/dynamic-values-v2/experiments/d9eecebd-8cd6-4a05-8687-490b1db1e454?tab=history) that controlled what percentage of traffic was routed through the affected code path. The issue was surfaced Mar 6 through an [LCM blender weight analysis](https://github.com/doordash/post_experiment_deep_dive/blob/master/project/HP_Blender/hp_blender_v0.1/analysis_docs/lcm_store_carousel_logging_bug.md) that found a \~7.3x gap in store carousel events per request (1.85 with LCM vs 13.54 without), prompting the backend investigation that identified the root cause.

### Why It Happened

A refactor ("[Add FDM support for Deals for you carousel](https://github.com/doordash/feed-service/pull/56553)", released Jan 21, 2026\) migrated store carousel serialization to a new shared builder but omitted a required parameter, causing facet IDs to be generated without the `store_carousel:` infix (e.g. `carousel.standard:favorites` instead of `carousel.standard:store_carousel:favorites`).

The Iguazu logging pipeline in `IguazuEventUtil` routes each facet to the correct event generator by string-matching the facet id against numerous hardcoded prefix constants (e.g. `"carousel.standard:store_carousel"`, `"carousel.standard:deal_carousel"`, etc.). Once facet IDs no longer contained the `store_carousel:` infix, the unrecognized facet matched no branch in the `when` expression and was silently skipped, no error, no metric, no log. The coupling between serialization and logging is entirely implicit, enforced only by string equality at runtime.

### How It Was Fixed

The missing `facetIdPrefix` parameter was restored in `StoreCarouselMapper`, bringing facet IDs back to the expected `carousel.standard:store_carousel:<carouselId>` format. A regression test was added asserting the exact facet ID format. This change was released Mar 11, 2026 via [PR](https://github.com/doordash/feed-service/pull/60973).

### Impact

*Estimates based on a pre-regression baseline ratio (store carousel events per request, Jan 15–19, 2026). Lost \= (actual requests × baseline ratio) − actual events. Computed across all 24 hours. See Appendix for SQL and full daily breakdown.*

**An estimated \~32 billion store carousel Iguazu events were not emitted over the 49-day window, Jan 21 – Mar 10, 2026\.** Downstream ML pipelines using store carousel rows from this window may have trained on incomplete data (\~5.5 non-store\_list carousels/request on affected traffic vs \~15.6 on unaffected). Teams should assess exposure and consider re-running against clean data post-fix.

Below is a table of breakdown of estimated lost store\_carousel events correlated against Andromeda DV state:

| Phase | [Andromeda DV](https://ops.doordash.team/decision-systems/dynamic-values-v2/experiments/d9eecebd-8cd6-4a05-8687-490b1db1e454?tab=history) State | Est. Lost | % Missing |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Jan 21–31 | Android 100%, iOS hitch fix 25%; iOS Users ramped 0%→100% Jan 28–29 | \~2.8B | 4–27% |
| Feb 1–11 | iOS Users at 100%; iOS hitch fix ramped 25%→100% (Feb 9–10) | \~3.1B | 8–42% |
| Feb 12–25 | iOS hitch fix holdout removed → full treatment on broken path | \~15B | 53–84% |
| Feb 26–28 | `hide_delivery_fee` segment rolled to 100% then back to 0% | \~2.4B | 46–70% |
| Mar 1–10 | `hide_delivery_fee` removed, back to full broken traffic | \~8.9B | 48–81% |
| **Total** |  | **\~32B** | **\~45%** |

### Open Decisions: Backfill (P0)

- Identify impacted teams and evaluate if backfill is needed based on above impact  
- Determine which ML models and pipelines consumed store carousel data during the Jan 21 – Mar 10 window and assess whether a backfill of the missing events is necessary  
  - What is the cost of not backfilling?  
- If backfill is needed, figure out if there is an existing process for it or if it needs to be discovered

### Prevention

This regression took 49 days from introduction to detection (Jan 21 → Mar 6\) and another 5 days to root-cause and fix (Mar 6 → Mar 10). The core issue is a lack of alerts and observability on the logging pipeline.

#### Future mitigation actions we should take

- Add monitoring on all event volume types relative to a rolling baseline, a per-request drop in a specific `facet_type` should trigger an alert for example.  
- Ensure all conditional logic performed in *feed-service* when emitting these logs have an else {} clause with monitoring that can catch any misses between string comparisons when determining what events to log. [Example](https://github.com/doordash/feed-service/blob/master/libraries/domain-util/src/main/kotlin/com/doordash/consumer/feed/domainUtil/iguazu/IguazuEventUtil.kt#L348) where this change is needed  
- Reduce structural brittleness by not relying on the serialized homepage data and introduce logging contracts so mismatches are caught at compile time, not runtime.

# Appendix

## A1. Impact Estimation SQL

```sql

WITH baseline_period AS (
    SELECT
        SUM(CASE WHEN facet_type = 'store_carousel' AND horizontal_position_in_facet = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 1.0 /
        NULLIF(APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT(request_id), 0) AS baseline_ratio
    FROM IGUAZU.SERVER_EVENTS_PRODUCTION.CX_CROSS_VERTICAL_HOME_PAGE_FEED_ICE
    WHERE iguazu_partition_date BETWEEN '2026-01-15' AND '2026-01-19'
),
daily_performance AS (
    SELECT
        iguazu_partition_date,
        APPROX_COUNT_DISTINCT(request_id) AS actual_requests,
        COUNT(CASE WHEN facet_type = 'store_carousel' AND horizontal_position_in_facet = 0 THEN 1 END) AS actual_carousels
    FROM IGUAZU.SERVER_EVENTS_PRODUCTION.CX_CROSS_VERTICAL_HOME_PAGE_FEED_ICE
    WHERE iguazu_partition_date >= '2026-01-21'
    GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
    p.iguazu_partition_date,
    p.actual_requests,
    p.actual_carousels,
    ROUND(p.actual_requests * b.baseline_ratio, 0) AS expected_carousels,
    ROUND((p.actual_requests * b.baseline_ratio) - p.actual_carousels, 0) AS estimated_lost_carousels,
    ROUND(100 * (1 - (p.actual_carousels / NULLIF(p.actual_requests * b.baseline_ratio, 0))), 2) AS percent_missing
FROM daily_performance p
CROSS JOIN baseline_period b
ORDER BY 1;
```

## A3. LCM Carousel Correlation ([Link](https://github.com/doordash/post_experiment_deep_dive/blob/master/project/HP_Blender/hp_blender_v0.1/analysis_docs/lcm_store_carousel_logging_bug.md))

*Source: LCM blender weight analysis, Mar 6, 2026\. Data from Mar 5, 2026 — 0.1% session sample (\~46.8K requests).*

Requests containing LCM carousels were routed through the new FDM/`StoreCarouselMapper` code path (the broken path). Requests without LCM were still on the old path with correct facet IDs. The gap in `store_carousel` events per request directly reflects the split between broken and healthy code paths.

| Metric | Requests with LCM (55%, n=25,494) | Requests without LCM (45%, n=21,281) |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Avg `store_carousel` per request | 1.85 | 13.54 |
| Avg `store_list` per request | 54.8 | 52.6 |
| Avg `deal_list` per request | 1.00 | 1.00 |
| Avg `item_carousel` per request | 2.67 | 1.09 |

A single-request drill-down (request `d1deee2d-062e-4c8b-b448-fcab93231613`) confirmed the pattern: 2 LCM carousels present, zero `store_carousel` entries in the log, all other entity types (`store_list`, `deal_list`, `reels_carousel`) present and unaffected.

## A4. DV Revision History

DV: [andromeda\_homepage\_store\_carousel](https://ops.doordash.team/decision-systems/dynamic-values-v2/experiments/d9eecebd-8cd6-4a05-8687-490b1db1e454?tab=history)

| Revision | Author | Description | Date |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 96 | Byran Yang | Segment 'iOS hitch fix w/ Holdout' distribution: Treatment w/ Holdout → Equal Weights, No Holdout | 2025-12-18 |
| 97 | Ephraim Russo | Rollout Android Users from 0% to 100% | 2025-12-19 |
| 98 | Ephraim Russo | Rollout iOS hitch fix w/ Holdout from 0% to 25% | 2025-12-19 |
| 99 | Byran Yang | Rollout iOS Users from 0% to 1% | 2026-01-28 |
| 100 | Byran Yang | Rollout iOS Users from 1% to 5% | 2026-01-28 |
| 101 | Byran Yang | Rollout iOS Users from 5% to 10% | 2026-01-28 |
| 102 | Byran Yang | Rollout iOS Users from 10% to 50% | 2026-01-29 |
| 103 | Byran Yang | Rollout iOS Users from 50% to 100% | 2026-01-29 |
| 104 | Kang Cheng | Rollout iOS hitch fix w/ Holdout from 25% to 50% | 2026-02-09 |
| 105 | Kang Cheng | Rollout iOS hitch fix w/ Holdout from 50% to 100% | 2026-02-10 |
| 106 | Byran Yang | Segment 'iOS hitch fix w/ Holdout' distribution: Equal Weights, No Holdout → 100% to treatment | 2026-02-12 |
| 107 | Josh Woods | Update dynamic value (revision 106 → 107\) | 2026-02-25 |
| 108 | Siyu Meng | Update dynamic value (revision 107 → 108\) | 2026-02-26 |
| 109 | Byran Yang | Segment added: hide\_delivery\_fee — Lifecycle: RUNNING → AWAITING\_RAMP | 2026-02-26 |
| 110 | Byran Yang | Rollout hide\_delivery\_fee from 0% to 100% | 2026-02-26 |
| 111 | Byran Yang | Update dynamic value (revision 110 → 111\) | 2026-02-26 |
| 112 | Byran Yang | Rollback hide\_delivery\_fee from 100% to 50% | 2026-02-27 |
| 113 | Byran Yang | Rollback hide\_delivery\_fee from 50% to 0% | 2026-02-27 |
| 114 | Byran Yang | Segment removed: hide\_delivery\_fee — Lifecycle: AWAITING\_RAMP → RUNNING | 2026-03-02 |

### 

### A4. Drop in HP discovery\_organic\_engagement\_instance\_v1

Key observations:  
  \- Jan 12–Feb 8: High volume, ranging 150–221M rows/day, with a peak on Jan 17 (\~221M)  
  \- Feb 9 onward: Sharp drop to 60–85M rows/day — roughly a 3x reduction that has persisted through March  
  \- The drop is abrupt (single day), suggesting a pipeline change, data filtering, or upstream schema change rather than organic traffic decline  
  \- Mar 11 partial spike (\~140M) is likely an in-progress backfill or the day not yet complete when queried

![][image1]

## A5. ML Features Effected

All the features which has the discovery\_organic\_engagement\_instance\_v1 as upstream table

| Feature Category | Feature Name | Number of Features | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| **CAROUSEL FEATURES** | cx\_carousel\_seq\_features | 7 | organic clicked/converted carousel sequences |
|  | consumer\_carousel\_interactions\_hp\_v0 | 7 | views, clicks, conversions, CTR, CVR 28d |
|  | carousel\_aggregated\_features | 21 | views, clicks, conversions, CTR, CVR at 28d/84d/168d |
|  | carousel\_id\_encoder | 5 | carousel ID encodings |
| **CONSUMER DISTANCE & SENSITIVITY** | consumer\_distance\_sensitivity | 24 | clicked/converted store ETA & distance means |
|  | consumer\_eta\_elasticity | 12 | ETA elasticity, fast order share, prefers fast flag |
|  | consumer\_long\_distance | 16 | avg long distance fee, order rate, distance percentiles |
|  | consumer\_fee\_sensitivity | 12 | delivery fee elasticity, fee percentiles |
|  | consumer\_store\_long\_distance\_fee | 1 |  |
| **VARIABLE PROFIT** | variable\_profit\_features | 16 | avg variable profit at consumer, store, CS, daypart level |
|  | consumer\_store\_subtotal | 8 | avg subtotal at consumer, store, CS, daypart level |
|  | commission\_miles\_tradeoff | 12 | avg commission fee, per mile at consumer, store, CS, daypart level |
| **EMBEDDINGS & RANKERS** | district\_consumer\_embeddings | 5 | food taxonomy, cuisine, catalog embeddings |
|  | exploration\_ranker\_production\_pipeline | 8 | LinUCB exploration params |
